title: AWS Data Science Transition: SageMaker End-to-End Workflow

selected_videos.title: AI Engineering with AWS SageMaker: Crash Course for Beginners!
selected_videos.curriculum_role: Substantial SageMaker foundation and orientation before building the project
selected_videos.inclusion_reason: The title indicates a substantial beginner-oriented SageMaker course. It provides the strongest available foundation for a learner without professional cloud experience, although its exact coverage should be verified because the evidence is metadata-only.

selected_videos.title: How to upload files to S3 using Python | Create Users in AWS | IAM Access | DE Project Part 3
selected_videos.curriculum_role: AWS access and initial storage setup
added_topics: AWS IAM user creation and access
selected_videos.inclusion_reason: The title suggests hands-on practice with IAM access management and S3 uploads, addressing foundational permissions and dataset-storage needs. Coverage of roles, policies, and SageMaker execution roles should be verified.

selected_videos.title: AWS CLI Tutorial | Learn to run AWS CLI commands in 10 minutes
selected_videos.inclusion_reason: The title explicitly identifies a practical AWS CLI tutorial, giving the learner the requested command-line exposure before working through AWS resources. Its coverage of profiles, regions, and data-science-specific commands should be verified.

selected_videos.title: Introduction to Boto3 for Python (Upload File to AWS S3)
added_topics: Uploading files to S3 from Python
selected_videos.inclusion_reason: The title explicitly focuses on using Boto3 for Python-based S3 file uploads, adding the requested programmatic AWS access alongside the CLI. The narrow video should be supplemented by the main project for downloads, SageMaker operations, and artifact handling.

selected_videos.title: From Laptop to Cloud: A Complete End-to-End Data Science Project with AWS SageMaker
selected_videos.curriculum_role: Primary end-to-end AWS data-science project spine
added_topics: Moving a data-science workflow from a laptop to AWS
Cloud-based model development and deployment context
selected_videos.inclusion_reason: The title identifies a complete end-to-end data-science project moving from a laptop to AWS SageMaker, making it the strongest apparent match for the learner's practical goal. S3 storage, IAM, training jobs, artifacts, boto3, and endpoint prediction testing are not confirmed by metadata and should be verified.

selected_videos.title: Sagemaker Tutorial - 1 | Build a Sklearn Model with AWS Sagemaker & Custom Training Script
selected_videos.curriculum_role: Gap-filling implementation resource for custom model training
selected_videos.inclusion_reason: The title explicitly focuses on building a scikit-learn model with AWS SageMaker and a custom training script. It adds concrete training implementation depth that the broader project may compress, though its use of a real dataset and artifact handling should be verified.

covered_topics: Scikit-learn SageMaker training with a custom script
Cloud transition from local development to AWS
uncovered_topics: Core AWS regions, availability zones, accounts, and systematic service selection
IAM roles, policies, and SageMaker execution-role design beyond possible user access coverage
Detailed AWS CLI installation, profiles, regions, and resource-specific commands
S3 prefixes, downloads, dataset organization, and model-artifact storage
Boto3 operations for SageMaker jobs, models, endpoints, and invocation
SageMaker processing workflows for preparing a real dataset
Explicit packaging of training outputs and saving model artifacts to S3
Creating a SageMaker model from S3 artifacts
Explicit endpoint invocation and test predictions
Testing, debugging, monitoring, and diagnosing IAM, S3, packaging, or deployment errors
limitations: The selected set totals approximately 345.0 minutes, which is within the preferred duration range and hard budget; the duration calculation is based on supplied metadata.
Candidate 17 is the strongest apparent project spine, but metadata does not verify that it includes every required stage, a real dataset, or reproducible endpoint predictions.
Candidate 15 supplies substantial foundation time but has broad crash-course framing and unverified topic coverage; some material may overlap with the project spine.
The pool has no clearly evidenced resource dedicated to SageMaker processing, model-artifact packaging, endpoint invocation, or systematic debugging.
CLI and Boto3 coverage are represented by short focused supplements, but their depth and AWS data-science relevance should be verified before study.
